title: sanjaysgk/ipg pipeline parameters
description: Immunopeptidomics Cryptic peptide databse construction from RNAseq

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row. See [usage docs](https://github.com/sanjaysgk/ipg/usage#samplesheet-input).

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

reference_genome_options.title: Reference genome options
reference_genome_options.description: Reference genome related files and options required for the workflow.

test_bundle.description: Path to a pre-built chr22 test data bundle (see scripts/build_test_bundle.sh); used by the test profile to skip large downloads.

genome.help_text:
If using a reference genome configured in the pipeline using iGenomes, use this parameter to give the ID for the reference. This is then used to build the full paths for all required reference genome files e.g. `--genome GRCh38`. 

See the [nf-core website docs](https://nf-co.re/usage/reference_genomes) for more details.

fasta.description: Path to reference genome FASTA (may be .gz; PREPARE_GENOME decompresses automatically).
fasta.help_text: The reference genome FASTA used for alignment, variant calling, and FastaAlternateReferenceMaker. If the path ends in .gz the pipeline decompresses it before indexing. Auto-resolved from --genome if set.

fasta_fai.description: Path to samtools-faidx index (.fai). Built automatically if not provided.

fasta_dict.description: Path to GATK sequence dictionary (.dict). Built automatically if not provided.

star_index.description: Path to the STAR index directory. Built automatically if not provided (~32 GB RAM, 30-60 min).

gtf.description: Reference gene annotation GTF (may be .gz; decompressed automatically).

rseqc_bed.description: BED file for RSeQC infer_experiment.py. Auto-resolved from --genome if set.

dbsnp.description: dbSNP known-variants VCF for BQSR. Auto-resolved from --genome if set.

known_indels.description: Known indels VCF for BQSR. Auto-resolved from --genome if set.

mills.description: Mills gold-standard indels VCF for BQSR. Auto-resolved from --genome if set.

germline_resource.description: Germline resource for Mutect2. Auto-resolved from --genome if set.

igenomes_ignore.description: Do not load the iGenomes reference config.
igenomes_ignore.help_text: Do not load `igenomes.config` when running the pipeline. You may choose this option if you observe clashes between custom parameters and those supplied in `igenomes.config`.

igenomes_base.description: The base path to the igenomes reference files

save_reference.description: Save built/downloaded reference files to outdir/reference/ for reuse in future runs.
save_reference.help_text: When using --genome GRCh38, the pipeline downloads and builds reference files (FASTA, GTF, STAR index, etc.) into the Nextflow work directory, which is temporary. Set --save_reference to copy these files to ${outdir}/reference/ so you can point to them in future runs with explicit --fasta, --star_index, etc. params instead of re-downloading.

include_variant_peptides.description: Include alt-reference variant-derived peptides in the cryptic peptide DB.
include_variant_peptides.help_text: When true, the db_construct subworkflow runs the unmasked + indel branches (FastaAlternateReferenceMaker -> revert_headers -> alt_liftover -> gffread -> triple_translate) in addition to the reference-only branch, and squishes all three peptide sets together into the final cryptic.fasta. When false (default) only the reference branch runs and squish receives one input — matching the legacy Scull et al. 2021 behaviour as observed in D122_Lung. This is a pipeline-level flag (one run = one intent); to mix modes for a heterogeneous cohort, run the pipeline twice. NOTE: Variant calling is ALWAYS performed in tumour-only Mutect2 mode against a gnomAD-style germline allele-frequency database; this pipeline does NOT support matched tumour-normal calling. Set to true only when the sample is expected to harbour biologically meaningful somatic variants worth incorporating into the MS/MS search space (e.g. tumour tissue, hypermutated cell lines, MMR-deficient samples). For normal tissue, cell lines, or any sample where variant peptides would mostly add noise, leave at false.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

ms_search_options.title: MS search options
ms_search_options.description: Parameters for the MS database search step (--step ms_search).

ms_input.description: Samplesheet CSV for MS data (required when --step ms_search).

search_fasta.description: Cryptic FASTA database for MS search (output from --step db_construct).

canonical_fasta.description: Local canonical proteome FASTA (gzipped or plain). Takes precedence over the download. When unset, DOWNLOAD_UNIPROT_PROTEOME fetches --canonical_proteome_url. Ignored when --db_search_mode is 'cryptic_only'.

canonical_proteome_url.description: URL of the gzipped canonical proteome, downloaded and cached by DOWNLOAD_UNIPROT_PROTEOME when --canonical_fasta is not given. Default = UniProt human reference proteome UP000005640.

db_search_mode.description: How canonical and cryptic are searched. 'appended': one combined DB with class-separated FDR (recommended). 'separate': independent searches reconciled downstream (follow-up). 'cryptic_only': cryptic DB alone.

ms_engines.description: Comma-separated list of MS search engines to use: msfragger, comet, sage, peaks.

msfragger_jar.description: Path to MSFragger JAR file (academic license, user must download).

msfragger_mem.description: Memory in GB for MSFragger JVM heap.

msfragger_split.description: Number of MSFragger split-database chunks. 1 = standard single search; >1 = split-database search (for large no-enzyme cryptic DBs that OOM the fragment index), with full-DB e-value recompute so results are statistically identical to an unsplit search. Requires the real MSFragger (--msfragger_jar or --msfragger_license).

instrument.description: Instrument type. Auto-detected from mzML if not specified.

mod_type.description: Modification search type for MS engines.

peaks_psm_csv.description: PEAKS Studio db.psms.csv export. Required when 'peaks' is in --ms_engines; spectrum indices are resolved against CONVERT_MZML's index2scan pickles.

peaks_min_match_fraction.description: Minimum fraction of PEAKS rows that must resolve to scan numbers or the run aborts.

ms2rescore_engine.description: Rescoring engine MS2Rescore uses for final FDR. 'mokapot' runs in-process; 'percolator' shells out to the percolator CLI.

ms2rescore_write_report.description: Write the MS2Rescore HTML QC report. Off by default because the DeepLC RT scatter aborts when retention-time predictions are sparse (small or low-ID databases).

mokapot_train_fdr.description: mokapot INTERNAL model-training FDR (not the reported FDR). Raise toward 0.5 for small or low-ID databases where mokapot cannot calibrate at a tight threshold; final FDR control is integrate_fdr.

mokapot_test_fdr.description: mokapot INTERNAL score-calibration FDR (not the reported FDR). Raise toward 0.5 for small or low-ID databases; mokapot aborts with 'no target PSMs below eval_fdr' if it is too tight.

msfragger_license.description: MSFragger academic license key (required when using the bioconda MSFragger wrapper).

skip_ms2rescore.description: Bypass the MS2Rescore step and integrate raw engine scores instead.

skip_integrate_engines.description: Bypass INTEGRATE_ENGINES (implied when skip_ms2rescore is true).

integrate_fdr.description: Final per-class FDR threshold applied in INTEGRATE_ENGINES — this is the reported FDR.

canonical_protein_prefixes.description: Comma-separated accession prefixes marking canonical proteins. Peptides mapping only to non-canonical proteins are classed 'cryptic' by INTEGRATE_ENGINES.

run_pepquery.description: Run PepQuery2 spectrum-level validation on the cryptic peptide subset; annotates pepquery_status onto the integrated peptides.

cryptic_min_spec_pearson.description: Minimum MS2PIP spectral (fragment-intensity) Pearson correlation for a cryptic peptide to be flagged spectral_status=pass. Unset = annotate only.

cryptic_max_rt_diff.description: Maximum DeepLC retention-time error for a cryptic peptide to be flagged spectral_status=pass. Unset = annotate only.

run_annotate_origin.description: Backtrack cryptic peptides to their RNA-seq source ORF/transcript/gene/locus/novelty-class using the 3-frame translation + gffcompare .tracking (no network).

origin_orf_fasta.description: 3-frame translated ORF FASTA (TRIPLE_TRANSLATE output) used by --run_annotate_origin.

annotate_with_ensembl.description: Add rich region/frame/biotype origin calls (5'UTR/3'UTR/CDS/intron, in/out-of-frame, ENSG, Ensembl coords) via the origins Ensembl pass. Set false for a reproducible, network-free run using local annotate_origin only.

origin_transcriptome.description: Reference transcriptome FASTA (origins -n) for the Ensembl region-call pass. Defaults to DB_CONSTRUCT's transcriptome output when run end-to-end.

origin_tracking.description: gffcompare .tracking (origins -r) for the Ensembl region-call pass. Defaults to TRANSCRIPT_ASSEMBLY's tracking output when run end-to-end.

run_cryptic_report.description: Generate a self-contained cryptic-discovery HTML report (class breakdown, cross-engine corroboration, PepQuery/spectral confidence, novelty class, origin, rescore shift).

immunoinformatics_options.title: Immunoinformatics options
immunoinformatics_options.description: Downstream analysis of integrated peptides: HLA binding, motif clustering, quantification, host background check. Each tool is individually gated.

run_blastp_host.description: Run blastp-short against a host/contaminant BLAST DB.

gibbs_clusters.description: 'auto' for [2-5], or explicit integer/range string like '[2-5]'.

blast_db.description: Path prefix to a pre-built blastp database (e.g. /path/human).

host_species.description: Upper-case host species tag used to filter the species column before blastp.

post_ms_options.title: Post-MS analysis options
post_ms_options.description: Parameters for the post-MS database search analysis step (--step post_ms).

step.description: Pipeline entry point. 'db_construct' runs full DB construction from FASTQ. 'ms_search' runs MS database search with open-source engines. 'post_ms' runs post-MS search analysis only.

post_ms_input.description: Samplesheet CSV for post-MS analysis (required when --step post_ms).

uniprot_fasta.description: Path to UniProt FASTA database for origins analysis.

transcriptome_fasta.description: Path to the reference transcriptome FASTA from DB construction (gffread output).

prefix_tracking.description: Path to the gffcompare .tracking file from DB construction.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

variant_annotation_options.title: Variant annotation options
variant_annotation_options.description: Configure VEP / SnpEff / AlphaMissense annotation of Mutect2 calls. Published leaf branch; does not feed the cryptic-peptide database.

tools.description: Comma-separated annotators to run. Empty/null skips vcf_annotate_all entirely.

vep_cache.description: Pre-staged VEP cache dir; auto-downloaded if unset.

snpeff_cache.description: Pre-staged SnpEff cache dir; auto-downloaded if unset.

alphamissense_tsv.description: Path to AlphaMissense_hg38.tsv.gz (sibling .tbi expected). Optional VEP plugin; ignored if 'alphamissense' not in --tools.
